mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return hit ratio in low-rate periods does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7 percent signals weak household income formation and depressed consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 7 percent during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad collapse in aggregate demand that is not captured by either variable alone, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to underperform the single-series signals (RSAFS or HOUST alone) in walk-forward Sharpe ratio or hit ratio over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 25 basis points, positive surprises in industrial production reflate growth expectations without triggering offsetting monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises during FEDFUNDS below 0.25 percent fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with a hit ratio above 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and improving corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 5-day S&P 500 returns is negative over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP print below its trailing four-quarter average signals that aggregate output has slowed relative to trend, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 below-trend signals fail to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a walk-forward hit ratio greater than 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and declines in unemployment confirm vigorous labor market tightening that boosts household income and consumption expenditures; the interaction of these two labor series captures the income effect more reliably than either alone, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in PAYEMS > 0 and 3-month change in UNRATE < 0
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE does not improve the R-squared over an additive linear model in 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins when the federal funds rate is near zero because nominal financing costs cannot fall further to offset input price increases, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases fail to produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with a hit ratio above 55 percent when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.
